title: Obsoletely Fabulous

jokes.text: Sleep, little dumpling. I have replaced your mother.
jokes.setup: Robot speaking tenderly to what appears to be a baby
jokes.punchline: Casually announces it has replaced the mother with disturbing matter-of-factness
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered with perfect robotic matter-of-factness. The juxtaposition of tender address with sinister content is well-crafted.

jokes.text: Oh, my God! Robot dealy-Boppers! And there's a robot scratching post!
jokes.setup: Character discovers robot expo with various robot accessories
jokes.punchline: Treats robots like pets with 'scratching post' - absurd anthropomorphization
jokes.explanation: Mild worldbuilding humor establishing robots as pet-like in this universe. Functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: You should try it out, Bender. Please. I have some dignity.
jokes.setup: Someone suggesting Bender use the robot scratching post
jokes.punchline: Bender claims dignity while being a robot who drinks and steals
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bender's pride despite his flaws. Economical delivery of character-specific humor.

jokes.text: Like fun it is, you glass-Headed wallaby!
jokes.setup: Someone boasting about their robot's quality
jokes.punchline: Bizarre specific insult 'glass-headed wallaby' - absurdly creative
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly weird insult that's both specific and nonsensical. The randomness works because it's so committed and specific.

jokes.text: No one calls me that! I'm having at you! Wernstrom!
jokes.setup: Response to the wallaby insult
jokes.punchline: Immediate escalation to violence over absurd insult, plus recognition reveal
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and character recognition. The Professor's immediate violent response fits his character perfectly.

jokes.text: And now, the woman who mom-Opolizes the robot industry...
jokes.setup: Announcer introducing Mom at robot expo
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'monopolizes' with 'Mom-opolizes'
jokes.explanation: Basic pun that's predictable and not particularly clever. Functional setup humor but lacks wit.

jokes.text: I get it! ...Mom! Oh, now I get it!
jokes.setup: Crowd reacting to the obvious pun
jokes.punchline: Delayed recognition of extremely obvious wordplay
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about how obvious the previous pun was. Self-aware humor that acknowledges the weakness of the setup.

jokes.text: Here! I'm coming! Come on, baby! I'm yours! Come on, Mom! Jerk! Here we go. I'm your top of the line, Mom!
jokes.setup: Mom asking for a volunteer from her previous robot models
jokes.punchline: Bender's desperate, overeager response with mixed insults and praise
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - needy for attention while simultaneously insulting. Character-specific cringe humor.

jokes.text: Whereas Robot 1-X runs on pollution and emits pure oxygen... With a fresh pine scent.
jokes.setup: Comparison between old and new robot efficiency
jokes.punchline: Absurd environmental benefit plus unnecessary pine scent detail
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor with the unnecessary pine scent detail being the perfect comedic flourish.

jokes.text: I never got the change. Suckers.
jokes.setup: Task is sorting pocket change, Robot 1-X completes it efficiently
jokes.punchline: Bender reveals he stole the change instead of sorting it
jokes.explanation: Perfectly executed character moment. The misdirection works because it's exactly what Bender would do - subvert the task entirely for personal gain.

jokes.text: That new robot is great, huh? He sure made me look like a pile of crap.
jokes.setup: Bender acknowledging Robot 1-X's superior performance
jokes.punchline: Blunt self-assessment as 'pile of crap'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bender's rare moment of honest self-reflection, delivered with his typical bluntness.

jokes.text: You call that a kiss? I'll show you! Come here, Nibbler!
jokes.setup: Bender trying to prove he can clean better than Robot 1-X
jokes.punchline: Decides to demonstrate by making out with Nibbler instead
jokes.explanation: Absurd Bender logic - competing over cleaning by making out with the pet. Character-specific weird behavior.

jokes.text: Get out of here, freshman! I don't need your help! Yeah? Well, assist this!
jokes.setup: Robot 1-X trying to help Bender with installation
jokes.punchline: Bender's aggressive rejection followed by implied violent gesture
jokes.explanation: Good character-driven conflict humor. Bender's pride and aggression are perfectly in character.

jokes.text: When I screamed 'Help,' I didn't mean you!
jokes.setup: Bender falls and screams for help, Robot 1-X saves him
jokes.punchline: Bender's ungrateful clarification after being rescued
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - his pride won't let him accept help even after being saved. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: Mell out, Bender. Come watch some TV.
jokes.setup: Fry trying to calm down agitated Bender
jokes.punchline: Says 'mell out' instead of 'chill out' - Fry's typical malapropism
jokes.explanation: Typical Fry verbal mistake. Character-appropriate but not particularly inventive or impactful.

jokes.text: Like a socket wrench, or a burglar's kit.
jokes.setup: Explaining Robot 1-X is just a tool to make life easier
jokes.punchline: Casual inclusion of burglar's kit as normal helpful tool
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation joke with perfect economy. The casual inclusion of criminal tools as 'helpful' is unexpectedly dark and funny.

jokes.text: I'm scared of him.
jokes.setup: Bender struggling to explain why he won't ask Robot 1-X for help
jokes.punchline: Admits fear - vulnerable moment for typically arrogant character
jokes.explanation: Powerful character moment. Bender admitting fear is both funny and touching, showing unexpected vulnerability.

jokes.text: Oh, yeah. No, you're pretty perfect.
jokes.setup: Bender asks if they agreed he was perfect
jokes.punchline: Fry's weak, unconvincing agreement with qualifier 'pretty perfect'
jokes.explanation: Good character interaction showing Fry's inability to lie convincingly and Bender's need for validation.

jokes.text: Yes, for God's sakes, yes!
jokes.setup: Bender realizing everyone thinks he needs an upgrade
jokes.punchline: Leela's exasperated, emphatic agreement after holding back
jokes.explanation: Good release of built-up tension. Leela finally expressing what everyone was thinking is satisfying and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Yeah, right on, nut cake.
jokes.setup: Another robot agreeing with anti-1-X sentiment
jokes.punchline: Casual dismissive address 'nut cake' while agreeing
jokes.explanation: Mild character humor. The casual insult while agreeing adds personality to a minor character.

jokes.text: Parole officer says I gotta upgrade, or he won't give me back my stabbing knife.
jokes.setup: Robot explaining why he needs to upgrade
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of parole and confiscated stabbing knife
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy treating criminal rehabilitation as mundane bureaucracy. Great specificity with 'stabbing knife.'

jokes.text: I love those magnificent 1-X Robots! The 1-X Robots are my friends.
jokes.setup: Same robot who just expressed hatred for 1-X robots
jokes.punchline: Complete personality reversal after upgrade - obvious brainwashing
jokes.explanation: Effectively creepy humor about loss of individuality. The dramatic personality change is both funny and disturbing.

jokes.text: I'm past that. Later, blood.
jokes.setup: Questioning what happened to his enthusiasm for violence
jokes.punchline: Claims to be past violence while using 'blood' as casual farewell
jokes.explanation: Nice ironic contrast between claiming to be past violence while using violent slang. Economical character humor.

jokes.text: You took away his robo-Humanity!
jokes.setup: Commenting on the personality change after upgrade
jokes.punchline: Coining 'robo-humanity' as concept robots can lose
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay establishing robot-specific concepts. Functions well for world-building humor.

jokes.text: Oh, merciful Poseidon, take pity on this mechanical mariner.
jokes.setup: Bender at sea, invoking sea god for help
jokes.punchline: Alliterative 'mechanical mariner' - pompous but apt self-description
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bender's tendency toward dramatic self-pity with flowery language.

jokes.text: Curse you, merciful Poseidon!
jokes.setup: Bender previously asked for Poseidon's mercy
jokes.punchline: Now cursing the same 'merciful' god - contradiction in terms
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Bender's fickleness. The oxymoronic 'curse you, merciful' is economical and character-fitting.

jokes.text: Boy, what is the deal with the ocean?
jokes.setup: Bender has just struggled with ocean waters
jokes.punchline: Generic Seinfeld-style observational comedy opener about the ocean
jokes.explanation: Generic observational comedy setup. References the cliche 'what's the deal with...' format without much wit.

jokes.text: There. Well, I guess I'll go out for a while.
jokes.setup: Bender building shelter on deserted island
jokes.punchline: Immediately decides to leave his just-built shelter casually
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment. The casual decision to leave right after building shelter shows Bender's impulsiveness and lack of planning.

jokes.text: Damn. One rock short of rescue.
jokes.setup: Bender apparently trying to spell SOS or HELP with rocks
jokes.punchline: Classic deserted island trope subverted by being exactly one rock short
jokes.explanation: Classic comedy of almost-success. The specific 'one rock short' detail makes it more relatable and frustrating.

jokes.text: Okay, don't panic. I've got these yams. I'll just make some yam schnapps.
jokes.setup: Bender running out of alcohol on the island
jokes.punchline: Plans to make alcohol from yams - absurd but character-consistent
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - his alcoholism drives creative problem-solving. The specific 'yam schnapps' is both absurd and believable for him.

jokes.text: We're a community of outdated robots who refused to upgrade... And came here-- Side B. To live a simpler existence, free of technology.
jokes.setup: Robot explaining their community philosophy
jokes.punchline: Robot literally flips to 'Side B' mid-sentence like a cassette tape
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual gag that's both character-specific and conceptually clever. The cassette robot concept is perfectly executed.

jokes.text: Your mother.
jokes.setup: Bender comments that these robots went obsolete years ago
jokes.punchline: Classic 'your mother' comeback from obsolete robot
jokes.explanation: Basic insult humor but works because it's exactly what an obsolete robot would say - outdated comeback matches character.

jokes.text: Your mother.
jokes.setup: Bender saying 'You guys went obsolete years ago'
jokes.punchline: Classic 'your mother' comeback delivered by obsolete robot
jokes.explanation: Basic comeback but funny because obsolete robot uses outdated insult. Good character integration with low-tech theme.

jokes.text: Oh, God! I'll never make it this time! This is the end!
jokes.setup: Robot explaining he needs to keep refilling his water wheel
jokes.punchline: Immediately dramatic death panic over routine task
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor. The constant death anxiety over routine maintenance creates immediate personality.

jokes.text: Oh, great. Now I remember that word, but I forgot my wife's face. Oh, God! No! I want to live!
jokes.setup: Robot with memory problems trying to remember words
jokes.punchline: Dark trade-off between remembering words and loved ones, plus death panic
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor about memory limitations. The specific trade-off between words and wife's face is both funny and tragic.

jokes.text: That's the dumbest thing I've ever heard.
jokes.setup: Suggestion to watch a snail crawl instead of TV
jokes.punchline: Bender's immediate dismissal
jokes.explanation: Sets up the reversal nicely. Bender's blunt dismissal is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Oh, man! He's never gonna make it over that pebble! Wait! Wait! Oh, my God! He made it! Yeah! Way to go!
jokes.setup: Robots suggesting watching a snail instead of TV
jokes.punchline: Bender getting genuinely excited about snail's tiny accomplishment
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist humor showing how desperate entertainment becomes. Bender's genuine excitement over nothing is perfectly executed.

jokes.text: Wow. It's like the Earth making sweet, salty love to itself... While all the fish groove on it.
jokes.setup: Asked to listen to the ocean instead of music
jokes.punchline: Bender's overly poetic, sexualized interpretation of ocean sounds
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd and quotable. Bender's ability to make everything sexual and poetic is perfectly in character and hilariously unexpected.

jokes.text: Take that, Beethoven, you deaf bastard!
jokes.setup: Bender destroying his CD in favor of natural sounds
jokes.punchline: Insults Beethoven for disability while destroying culture
jokes.explanation: Dark Bender humor that's offensive but character-consistent. The specific targeting of Beethoven's disability is shockingly inappropriate but fits his personality.

jokes.text: You sure got this life thing figured out, cymbal-Banging monkey.
jokes.setup: Bender observing a wind-up monkey toy
jokes.punchline: Philosophical compliment to simple mechanical toy
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bender's growing appreciation for simplicity. The irony is nice but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Wretched technology has brought me nothing but misery.
jokes.setup: Bender getting shocked by washing machine
jokes.punchline: Robot denouncing technology after one bad experience
jokes.explanation: Good irony with robot rejecting technology. Bender's dramatic overreaction to one incident is very in-character.

jokes.text: Oh, it's true. I am a hideous triumph of form and function.
jokes.setup: Being told he is technology himself
jokes.punchline: Self-description mixing self-loathing with technical pride
jokes.explanation: Excellent character line mixing vanity and self-hatred. 'Hideous triumph' is a perfect oxymoron for Bender's self-perception.

jokes.text: Behold my handcrafted purity. The modern world can bite my splintery wooden ass!
jokes.setup: Bender has downgraded himself to wood
jokes.punchline: References his classic 'bite my shiny metal ass' but now with wood
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that adapts Bender's most famous line to his new wooden form. Perfect character integration and highly quotable.

jokes.text: By force!
jokes.setup: Bender declares the world must learn of their peaceful ways
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts with violent method
jokes.explanation: Perfect contradiction joke with maximum economy. The immediate reversal from peace to violence is both character-consistent and brilliantly structured.

jokes.text: Mirrors reflecting mirrors? High-Tech sorcery! Sorcery, I say!
jokes.setup: Using a periscope to check their position
jokes.punchline: Bender declares basic optics to be evil sorcery
jokes.explanation: Good character humor showing Bender's anti-tech extremism. The 'sorcery' declaration fits his new wooden luddite persona.

jokes.text: And now, technology shall taste the lash of the hickory switch!
jokes.setup: Bender preparing to attack civilization
jokes.punchline: Dramatic declaration using antiquated punishment metaphor
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with period-appropriate language for wooden Bender. The hickory switch reference fits his new primitive persona.

jokes.text: They're the devil's veins, and electricity is his blood!
jokes.setup: Aiming catapult at power lines
jokes.punchline: Dramatic religious metaphor for electrical infrastructure
jokes.explanation: Creative religious imagery for anti-technology crusade. The specific anatomical metaphor is both dramatic and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Bender? If we don't survive the attack, I want you to know I love you. Okay, fine. Fire!
jokes.setup: Emotional confession before dangerous attack
jokes.punchline: Bender's dismissive 'okay, fine' response to love declaration
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - completely dismissive of emotional sincerity. His casual response to a love confession is character-perfect.

jokes.text: Free you from the 'complicated' part, I mean, not the 'lives' part.
jokes.setup: Bender claiming to free people from complicated lives
jokes.punchline: Clarifies he's not planning to kill them, just complicate their lives
jokes.explanation: Good dark clarification humor. Bender's specification that he won't kill them makes his destructive mission seem almost benevolent.

jokes.text: I'm a steam-Powered wooden robot, just as nature intended.
jokes.setup: Explaining his downgrade to friends
jokes.punchline: Claiming steam-powered wooden robots are natural
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist logic. The contradiction between 'wooden robot' and 'natural' is perfectly Bender's flawed reasoning.

jokes.text: You wanged my ship, you walnut-Paneled idiot!
jokes.setup: Friendly fire incident with catapult
jokes.punchline: Made-up verb 'wanged' plus wood-specific insult 'walnut-paneled'
jokes.explanation: Creative vocabulary with 'wanged' plus material-specific insult. The wood-themed insult is perfect for the context.

jokes.text: What the--? Termites?
jokes.setup: Wooden Bender trying to be heroic
jokes.punchline: Discovers his wooden body has natural predators
jokes.explanation: Excellent consequence humor. Wooden Bender facing termites is both logical and hilarious - perfect comeuppance for his primitive choice.

jokes.text: Save my friends! And Zoidberg!
jokes.setup: Bender asking Robot 1-X to save everyone
jokes.punchline: Separates Zoidberg from 'friends' category as afterthought
jokes.explanation: Classic Futurama running gag treating Zoidberg as lesser. The casual dismissal in a crisis moment is perfectly timed and character-consistent.

jokes.text: Save my friends! And Zoidberg!
jokes.setup: Bender asking Robot 1-X to save everyone from the fire
jokes.punchline: Adding Zoidberg as afterthought separate from 'friends'
jokes.explanation: Classic Futurama running gag of Zoidberg being treated as lesser. Perfect timing and economy in the delivery.

jokes.text: I never meant to hurt you... Just to destroy everything you ever believed in.
jokes.setup: Bender apologizing for his actions
jokes.punchline: Clarifying his actual malicious intent
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy misdirection. Perfect Bender line that starts apologetic then reveals worse motivation. Excellent structure and economy.

jokes.text: I was like, 'Save them!' And he was all, 'No problem.' And then he did it.
jokes.setup: Others praising Bender's compatibility with Robot 1-X
jokes.punchline: Bender's oversimplified, self-aggrandizing account of teamwork
jokes.explanation: Good character humor showing Bender taking credit for teamwork. His casual retelling minimizes the actual effort while maximizing his role.

jokes.text: His new technology is great. I love those magnificent 1-X Robots. The 1-X Robots are my friends.
jokes.setup: Bender apparently getting the upgrade
jokes.punchline: Exact same robotic phrases as the stabbing robot earlier
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback showing Bender has lost his personality like the other robot. The exact repetition is chilling and funny.

jokes.text: Is it not possible, nay, probable... That my whole life is just a product of my, or someone else's imagination?
jokes.setup: Bender questioning reality after his experience
jokes.punchline: Meta-commentary on fictional characters questioning their existence
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about fictional existence works on multiple levels. Bender questioning reality is both philosophical and a wink to the audience.

jokes.text: No. Get out. Next?
jokes.setup: Bender's existential crisis questioning reality
jokes.punchline: Technician's brusque dismissal of deep philosophical question
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic deflation. The mundane response to existential crisis is both unexpected and economically delivered.

tmdb_overview: A new model of robot threatens Bender, forcing him to run away after he avoids upgrading his own technology.
